---
title: "About me"
toc: false
---



I studied Marine Sciences but part of what I understand about the ocean was learned through years of work at sea, fishing, sailing or diving for marine resources, crisscrossing ports, waves and mountains along the way. Over time, I returned to science, working at the intersection of marine ecology and geospatial analysis: an attempt to read patterns in coastal systems without losing sight of the textures that shape them. I explore the balance between field observations and remotely sensed products, with a particular interest seagrass meadows and macroalgal forests, landscapes often overlooked, yet fundamental to life along the shore.



![](assets/norway.JPG){.portrait}
